decisions.reasoning: Iron Hands threatens a 2HKO with Drain Punch, and Incineroar cannot effectively pressure it. Switching to Amoonguss provides a safe pivot that resists Fighting-type moves and can threaten to put the opponent to sleep or force a switch.
Iron Hands is likely to stay in to fish for an Ice Punch KO. Spore is the highest value play to neutralize the threat immediately, as Amoonguss survives any single hit from Iron Hands this turn.
Iron Hands is already asleep, but since I cannot KO it and it threatens a 2HKO with Ice Punch, refreshing the sleep status or forcing a switch is the safest play to maintain momentum.

Iron Hands is already asleep (as indicated by the HP status), so Spore is a wasted turn. However, since the opponent is likely to switch out to wake up or reset, and I have no immediate KO, Spore is the safest play to catch a switch-in or maintain pressure while I evaluate the board state.

Iron Hands is a significant threat with Life Orb, and Spore is the best way to neutralize it immediately. Even if they switch, putting a potential pivot to sleep provides massive momentum for the rest of the match.

Iron Hands is already asleep, but Spore is the only move that provides utility here. Since I cannot KO it and switching would give the opponent a free turn, I will use Spore to ensure it stays asleep or to reset the sleep counter if it wakes up.

Iron Hands is already asleep, but since I cannot KO it and it is a threat, re-applying Spore ensures it stays incapacitated while I maintain board control.

Amoonguss cannot threaten Flutter Mane and is being 3HKOed. Switching to Incineroar provides a safe pivot that resists both of Flutter Mane's STAB types, allowing me to scout for a Tera or force a switch.
Flare Blitz is a guaranteed OHKO on Flutter Mane, removing a major threat immediately. Since I can secure the KO this turn, I should prioritize the attack over switching.
